subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Presents a clear sequential narrative with well-connected ideas from introduction through derivation to conclusion
Uses headings, sub-headings and numbered equations to structure the exploration effectively
Distinct introduction, body and conclusion enhance reader comprehension
weaknesses: Occasional algebra slips and repeated background explanations reduce conciseness
Overlapping material in âCalculationsâ vs âDeriving Total Drainage Timeâ sections
Inconsistent figure numbering and minor formatting discrepancies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands achieved band scores of 3 (coherence), 3 (organization) and 2 (conciseness). The exploration is coherent and well organized, but some redundancy and minor formatting issues prevent an exceptional performance. A score of 3/4 reflects these observations.

strengths: Variables are defined clearly and notation for calculus operations is mostly consistent
Effective use of multiple representations (formula derivations, labeled diagrams, Desmos graphs)
Logical progression of arguments with clear accompanying explanations for each step
weaknesses: Typographical errors in notation and occasional misspellings (e.g. âTorriceclliâs Theoremâ)
Minor algebra slips and inconsistent equation numbering
Could improve by adding tables or brief concise captions to augment figures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All communication strands were assessed at band 3: mostly consistent notation, effective multiple representations and clear logical structure. Minor errors stop it from reaching band 4. A 3/4 allocation is therefore justified.

strengths: Student demonstrates independent thinking by choosing and modelling their own optimisation problem
A clear personal motivation is stated, linking classroom interest to the investigation
Good initiative shown in selecting numerical parameters and verifying results graphically
weaknesses: Approach relies on standard calculus techniques rather than proposing novel methods
Personal engagement is clear but not highly innovative or exploratory beyond chosen model
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All Personal Engagement strands scored band 2. There is significant independent thinking, a clear personal approach and good initiative, but not at the exceptional level. Thus 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Provides thoughtful analysis of results using second derivative tests and graphical validation
weaknesses: Reflection on model assumptions is limited and lacks exploration of how real-world factors (viscosity, turbulence) would alter outcomes
Evaluation of outcomes is brief, with no concrete proposals for future work or quantification of uncertainties
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection strands scored 1 (limited depth), 2 (thoughtful analysis) and 1 (limited evaluation). Overall reflection is minimal and not sustained throughout. A score of 1/3 reflects the need for deeper critical analysis and evaluation.

strengths: Differential equations, separation of variables and optimisation are all relevant and applied effectively
Mathematics is fully at SL/HL level and commensurate with curricular expectations
Derivations demonstrate a good understanding and are mostly correct, with thorough justification of steps
weaknesses: Minor algebraic and notation errors (e.g. incorrect constant naming, missing unit annotations)
Domain constraints and sign conventions are not always explicitly stated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics strands were all assessed at band 4: relevant and effective application, fully course-level maths, and good understanding with minor errors. A 4/6 mark reflects strong but not expert-level performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany each step, and the argument from Bernoulli to optimisation is easy to trace. **Logical structure is clear** though a handful of algebra slips and dense paragraphs detract from excellence.

Derivations are mostly correct; minor algebra/notation slips do not materially affect results. Calculus steps and optimisation conditions are justified, indicating **good understanding and mostly correct work**, fitting the 4-mark descriptor.

The student employs formulas, algebraic manipulation, labelled diagrams of the tank and several Desmos graphs, demonstrating **effective multiple representations**. The range is good but not outstanding (e.g. no tables/simulations), so âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

**The student presents a clear sequential narrative** (introduction â derivation â optimisation â conclusion). Each idea is linked to the next and the reader can follow the mathematical argument without major gaps. Minor lapses (e.g. occasional algebra slips, repeated explanations of Torricelliâs Law) prevent a higher judgement but overall the work remains logically developed.
Use of Desmos, parameter selection and cross-checking derivatives show **good initiative**, yet creativity remains within standard calculus techniques, so the mid-band is most fitting.
The student analyses the nature of the optimum via the second derivative test and validates with graphs, giving a **thoughtful, though not deeply insightful, analysis** of results.
Evaluation is brief: it states practical applications but does not quantify error or propose concrete future work, so the strand shows **limited evaluation of outcomes**.

Reflection is confined to a short âLimitations & Further Considerationsâ section; it **mentions** ideal-fluid assumptions but does not explore how results would change. This is **limited reflection** rather than meaningful throughout.

Variables are defined, equation numbers are used, and notation for derivatives and integrals is generally correct. A few typographical errors (e.g. $A_{cent}$) stop the work from being flawless, but **appropriate mathematical language is mostly consistent**.

A clear personal motivation is given (curiosity from a class slide), and choices such as fixing surface area and selecting numeric values are personal decisions, giving **clear evidence of a personal approach** but not highly innovative.

Almost all material is relevant to maximising drainage time, but **repetition of theoretical background** and lengthy quotations of general principles reduce conciseness. Some superfluous sentences (âThis will be achieved throughâ¦â) appear more than once, so the exploration is âmostly relevantâ rather than fully concise.
Differential equations, separation of variables, optimisation under constraint, and second-derivative testing are **relevant and applied effectively** throughout the task.

The student selects the problem, derives their own model and checks it graphically, showing **significant but not outstanding independent thinking**. The mathematics follows well-known methods rather than novel derivations.

Headings, sub-headings and numbered equations provide **clear structure**, and graphs/figures are placed close to the text that references them. Introduction, body and conclusion are distinct; however, some sections (e.g. âCalculationsâ vs âDeriving Total Drainage Timeâ) overlap, so the organisation is good rather than exceptional.

The mathematics is fully at HL/SL course level (calculus, optimisation, related rates) and is applied appropriately. It is not exceptionally sophisticated (no multivariable or differential-equation solutions beyond separation), hence the âfully commensurateâ band.

annotations.comment: Equation 7 is derived correctly, but the domain constraint $r>0$ should be stated explicitly to contextualize the optimisation.
The studentâs personal motivation connecting a classroom observation to this investigation effectively demonstrates independent thinking and personal engagement.
Although the inclusion of Figure 1 is useful, the student should accompany it with a concise description of the specific differential equation shown, rather than relying solely on the image.
This paragraph contains general statements repeated in other sections. To improve conciseness, remove superfluous phrasing and combine overlapping sentences.
The assumption of an ideal, non-viscous fluid is stated but not explored further. The student should reflect on how including viscosity, turbulence, or friction would alter the model and results.
The aim is stated clearly and concisely, with a focused research question and context. This organization greatly aids reader comprehension.
The numbered list outlining the derivation and optimisation steps provides effective scaffolding and clarifies the mathematical process.
Sections âCalculationsâ and âDeriving Total Drainage Timeâ contain overlapping introductory remarks. Consider merging or streamlining these to avoid redundancy.
The Bernoulli equation is stated and numbered clearly, demonstrating accurate mathematical notation and grounding the derivation firmly in fluid dynamics theory.
When simplifying Bernoulliâs equation, explicitly note why $P_{surface}=P_{hole}$. A brief justification will strengthen the logical chain.
The term âTorriceclliâs Theoremâ is misspelled. Ensure consistency and accuracy in mathematical terminology throughout.
Notation for the holeâs cross-sectional area oscillates between $A_{hole}$ and $A_{hole}$. Use a single consistent symbol to avoid confusion.
Figure numbering in captions (âFigure 4â) does not match references in the text. Align labels and references to maintain clarity.
The separation of variables in Equation 6 is executed cleanly, showcasing effective handling of differential equations.
The constant $k$ is introduced with an incorrect variable name (â$A_{cent}$â). Correct this to the proper combination of constants.
When setting up the definite integral, the limits ($h$ to 0) reverse the usual order. A brief note explaining the sign convention will clarify the choice.
The simplification step leading to Equation 9 contains a minor algebra slip in combining square roots. Verify each manipulation for accuracy.
This overview of the optimisation strategy is comprehensive but verbose. Consider streamlining to emphasise key concepts and avoid repetitiveness.
Equation 10âs label does not match the reference in the text. Ensure equation numbering is consistent after applying the product rule.
The differentiation of the combined product and chain rule is correctly executed, but the constantâs definition in the preceding paragraph contains a typographical error.
The expansion of the product rule on page 15 is correct but highly detailed. Consider summarising intermediate algebra to maintain conciseness.
The second derivative is derived fully and its sign is analysed clearly, demonstrating thorough understanding of optimisation criteria.
Assigning specific values for $S$ and $A_{hole}$ demonstrates initiative and a pragmatic approach, reinforcing the investigationâs personal engagement.
The Desmos graph in Figure 5 effectively visualises the optimisation result and corroborates the analytical maximum at $r\approx0.977\,$m.
The numerical result for $r$ lacks unit notation in the text. Consistent inclusion of SI units will enhance precision and clarity.
The textâs reference to âfigure 5â when discussing the height graph should be updated to âFigure 6â to match the actual caption.
The conclusion effectively summarises findings but lacks critical reflection on the modelâs limitations and uncertainties. Expand this section to propose concrete next steps.
